SEARCH
Module storm.core
Package st.orm.core.template

Interface Model<E extends Data,ID>

Type Parameters:
E - the type of the entity or projection.
ID - the type of the primary key, or Void for projections without a primary key.
All Known Implementing Classes:
ModelImpl
public interface Model<E extends Data,ID>
Represents the model of an entity or projection.

A model describes the structural mapping between a record type and its database representation. It exposes metadata such as schema, table/view name, and columns, and provides mechanisms to extract JDBC-ready values from entity or projection instances.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description

    Returns all columns of this model, including columns of expanded relationships.

    Collects declared column values into an ordered map.

    Finds a unique metamodel referring to the given entity type.

    Returns true if the given primary key represents a default value.

    Returns true if this model represents a joined inheritance entity (Joined Table inheritance), where the base table has common fields and each permitted subclass has its own extension table.

    Returns the name of the table or view.

    Returns the type of the primary key.

  • Method Details

    • schema

      String schema()
      Returns the schema, or an empty string if the schema is not specified.
      Returns:
      the schema, or an empty string if not specified.

    • name

      String name()
      Returns the name of the table or view.
      Returns:
      the table or view name.

    • qualifiedName

      String qualifiedName(@Nonnull SqlDialect dialect)
      Returns the qualified name of the table or view, including schema and quoting.
      Parameters:
      dialect - the SQL dialect used for quoting.
      Returns:
      the qualified table or view name.

    • type

      Class<E> type()
      Returns the Java type of the entity or projection.
      Returns:
      the record type.

    • isJoinedInheritance

      boolean isJoinedInheritance()
      Returns true if this model represents a joined inheritance entity (Joined Table inheritance), where the base table has common fields and each permitted subclass has its own extension table.
      Returns:
      true if this model uses joined table inheritance.
      Since:
      1.9

    • primaryKeyType

      Class<ID> primaryKeyType()
      Returns the type of the primary key.
      Returns:
      the primary key type, or Void.class if no primary key exists.

    • columns

      List<Column> columns()
      Returns all columns of this model, including columns of expanded relationships.

      The returned list is deterministic and stable. Declared columns are processed in declaration order. Foreign relationships are expanded depth-first at the position of the foreign-key column.

      Expanded columns always correspond to physical columns of the parent record. Join keys come from the parent row, not the referenced row.

      Returns:
      all columns of this model, including expanded relations.
      Since:
      1.7

    • declaredColumns

      List<Column> declaredColumns()
      Returns the columns declared directly on this model.

      Relationship expansion is not applied. The returned list preserves declared order.

      Index semantics: Column.index() refers to the index in columns(), not in this list.

      Returns:
      the declared columns of this model.
      Since:
      1.8

    • recordType

      RecordType recordType()
      Returns the record type metadata.
      Returns:
      the record type.
      Since:
      1.7

    • isDefaultPrimaryKey

      boolean isDefaultPrimaryKey(@Nullable ID pk)
      Returns true if the given primary key represents a default value.
      Parameters:
      pk - the primary key to check.
      Returns:
      true if the primary key is a default value.
      Since:
      1.2

    • getPrimaryKeyMetamodel

      Optional<Metamodel<E,ID>> getPrimaryKeyMetamodel()
      Returns the metamodel of the primary key, if present.
      Returns:
      the primary key metamodel, or empty if none exists.
      Since:
      1.7

    • getColumns

      List<Column> getColumns(@Nonnull Metamodel<?,?> metamodel) throws SqlTemplateException
      Resolves all columns associated with the given metamodel.
      Parameters:
      metamodel - the metamodel identifying one or more columns.
      Returns:
      the resolved columns.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException - if the metamodel is invalid or not present.
      Since:
      1.7

    • getSingleColumn

      default Column getSingleColumn(@Nonnull Metamodel<?,?> metamodel) throws SqlTemplateException
      Resolves a single column for the given metamodel.
      Parameters:
      metamodel - the metamodel identifying exactly one column.
      Returns:
      the resolved column.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException - if zero or multiple columns are resolved.
      Since:
      1.7

    • forEachValue

      void forEachValue(@Nonnull List<Column> columns, @Nonnull E record, @Nonnull BiConsumer<Column,Object> consumer) throws SqlTemplateException
      Iterates over the values of the given columns for the supplied record.

      Values are JDBC-ready. Conversions have already been applied.

      Ordering requirement: columns must be ordered according to the model's column order (usually columns() or declaredColumns()).

      Parameters:
      columns - the columns to extract values for, ordered in model column order.
      record - the record to extract values from.
      consumer - receives each column and its extracted value.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException - if extraction fails.
      Since:
      1.8

    • values

      default SequencedMap<Column,Object> values(@Nonnull List<Column> columns, @Nonnull E record) throws SqlTemplateException
      Collects column values into an ordered map.

      Ordering requirement: columns must be ordered according to the model's column order (usually columns() or declaredColumns()).

      Parameters:
      columns - the columns to extract values for.
      record - the record to extract values from.
      Returns:
      a map of columns to extracted values.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException - if extraction fails.
      Since:
      1.8

    • values

      default SequencedMap<Column,Object> values(@Nonnull E record) throws SqlTemplateException
      Collects all column values into an ordered map.

      This method is equivalent to values(List, Data) with columns().

      Parameters:
      record - the record to extract values from.
      Returns:
      a map of columns to extracted values.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException - if extraction fails.
      Since:
      1.8

    • declaredValues

      default SequencedMap<Column,Object> declaredValues(@Nonnull E record) throws SqlTemplateException
      Collects declared column values into an ordered map.

      The returned map preserves the iteration order of declaredColumns().

      Parameters:
      record - the record to extract values from.
      Returns:
      a map of declared columns to extracted values.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException - if extraction fails.
      Since:
      1.8

    • findMetamodel

      Optional<Metamodel<E,?>> findMetamodel(@Nonnull Class<? extends Data> type)
      Finds a unique metamodel referring to the given entity type.
      Parameters:
      type - the referenced entity type.
      Returns:
      the matching metamodel, or empty if not found or ambiguous.
      Since:
      1.8

    • forEachValue

      void forEachValue(@Nonnull Metamodel<E,?> metamodel, @Nonnull Object object, @Nonnull BiConsumer<Column,Object> consumer) throws SqlTemplateException
      Extracts values for a given metamodel and object.

      If object is a Data instance, the model extracts its id and maps columns from that id. Otherwise, object must be compatible with metamodel.fieldType().

      If the resolved value is null and the metamodel resolves to multiple columns, each column is emitted with null.

      Parameters:
      metamodel - the metamodel identifying the column(s).
      object - the value holder or entity instance.
      consumer - receives each resolved column and value.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException - if extraction fails.
      Since:
      1.8
